macro_segments.title: The Debate on Annulling Partial Vows and the Nazir Package Doctrine

micro_segments.title: Recap of Rav Yehuda amar Shmuel: Annulling a Vow Affecting Multiple Objects
Disagreement between Rabbi Yochanan and the Position of Rav Yehuda: Partial vs. Complete Annulment
The Nazir as a Package: No Partial Status, ein nezirus b'chatzayin
Abaye's Clarification: ein korban nezirus lachatziyin, No Partial Obligation for Offerings

macro_segments.title: The Case of the Woman Who Became Tamei During Nezirus and the Meaning of Partial Korban

micro_segments.title: The Baraita: Woman Vowed Nezirus, Became Tamei, Separated an Animal, Then Husband Annulled
The Gemara's Challenge: If Korban Only for Complete Nezirus, Why Does She Bring Chatas Ha-Of?
The Gemara's Dismissal: Special Case of Tumah, Not Proof of Partial Nezirus Korban
Deep Dive: Chatas Ha-Of on Safek (Yoledes) and Its Application to Partial Nezirus
The Gemara's Resolution: Chatas Ha-Of for Ambiguous Situations, Not Standard Korbanos

macro_segments.title: Return to the Core Debate: Can Husband Annul Only the Wine Part of Nezirus?

micro_segments.title: Re-presentation of the Baraita: Woman Vowed Nezirus, Became Tamei, Husband Annulled
The Gemara's Hypothetical: If Husband Annuls Only Wine (Inuy Nefesh), Does Tumah Part Remain?
Response: Tumah is Also Inuy Nefesh—Going to Funerals, Community Duties, Visiting Graves
Conclusion: Nezirus is a Package; Cannot Annul Wine Alone Without Tumah Falling Away

macro_segments.title: A Woman Forbids Herself Benefit from All Creatures: Konam She-Ani Nehenis L'Vriyos

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah: A Woman Vows She Won't Benefit from Any Creature; Husband Cannot Annul
Why Not Inuy Nefesh? The Ran's Explanation via the Chanvani (Storekeeper) Case
Direct vs. Indirect Benefit: The Husband as Intermediary
The Gemara's Question: If She Can Still Benefit from Husband, How is This Inuy Nefesh?

macro_segments.title: A Woman Forbids Herself Benefit from All Creatures: Konam She-Ani Nehenis L'Vriyos (continued)

micro_segments.title: Three Positions on Whether Husband is Included in 'All Creatures'

macro_segments.title: Position One (Ulla): Husband Not in Category of 'Creatures'; She Can Still Benefit from Him

micro_segments.title: Ulla's Answer: Husband Is Fundamentally Not Included in 'All People'
Support: She Can Also Benefit from Leket, Shichachah, Pe'ah

macro_segments.title: Position Two (Rava): Husband Included in 'All Creatures,' but Leket Shichachah Pe'ah Makes It Not Inuy Nefesh

micro_segments.title: Rava's Position: Husband Is Included; Only Leket Shichachah Pe'ah Prevents Annulment
The Ran's Problem with Rava: Why Not Apply This to the Storekeeper Case?
Possible Resolution: Storekeeper Case Only in Winter When Leket Unavailable

macro_segments.title: Position Three (Rav Nachman): Husband Excluded Only While Married; Falls Back If Divorced

micro_segments.title: Rav Nachman: Husband Not in 'All Creatures' as Long as Marriage Continues
How to Read the Mishnah: If Divorced, She Can Access Leket Shichachah Pe'ah
The Ran's Extended Discussion: Does Status Change Mid-Vow Require Reassessment?

macro_segments.title: Rava's Challenge to Rav Nachman via the Nituah Case: Sexual Pleasure Vow

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah on Sexual Vow: Woman Says She's Forbidden to Have Sexual Pleasure from Anyone
Rava's Logic: Why Must Husband Annul If He Weren't Included? This Proves He IS Included
The Husband Must Annul His Portion; She Remains Forbidden to Others
The Gemara's Resolution: In This Context, Husband Must Be Included or Vow Is Meaningless
Final Principle: Inclusion Depends on Whether 'All Creatures' Is Meaningful Without the Husband

topical_tags.term: Ein nezirus b'chatzayin (No Partial Nazir Status)
Ein korban nezirus lachatziyin (No Partial Offering)
Neder shehufar miktsato hufar kulo (Vow Annulled in Part Falls Entirely)
Meifer (Annul, Husband's Right to Annul Wife's Vow)
Devarim shebeino leveinah (Matters Between Husband and Wife)
Chatas Ha-Of (Offering of a Bird as Sin Offering)
Olas Ha-Of (Offering of a Bird as Burnt Offering)
Rabbi Yochanan vs. Rav Yehuda amar Shmuel
Nezir Shimshon (Nazirite Vow in Samson's Style)
Konam she-ani nehenis l'vriyos (Vow: I Forbid Myself Benefit from All Creatures)
Leket, Shichachah, Pe'ah (Gleanings, Forgotten Sheaves, Corner of Field)
